name: pystages

metadata.name: pystages
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 (LGPLv3)
metadata.summary: Motorized stage control library for scientific applications

documentation:
# PyStages

[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/pystages/badge/?version=latest)](https://pystages.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

PyStages is a Python 3 library for controlling motorized stages which have a
motion controller. It has been designed for microscopy test benches automation.

The following motion controllers are currently supported (only the main features
are implemented):

- Micos Corvus Eco controller
- New Scale Technologies M3-FS focus modules (serial only)
- Newport SMC100 motion controllers
- Tic Stepper Motor controller (USB only)
- CNC Router with GRBL/GCode instructions (CNC 3018-PRO)

The library also provides helper classes for basic vector manipulation and
autofocus computation.

## Documentation

Documentation is available on [Read the Docs](https://pystages.readthedocs.io).

## PyStages GUI

A user interface has been implemented to control the stages.

You can run it with the following command:

```bash
python -m pystages.gui
```

## Requirements

This library requires the following packages:

- [pyserial](https://pypi.org/project/pyserial/)
- [numpy](https://pypi.org/project/numpy/)
- [pyusb](https://pypi.org/project/pyusb/)
- [PyQt6](https://pypi.org/project/PyQt6/)

classes.name: pystages.autofocus.Autofocus

methods.name: pystages.autofocus.Autofocus.register
documentation:
Register a new focused point.

```
param x

Abscissa of the point.

param y

Ordinate of the point.

param z

Depth of the point.
```

methods.name: pystages.autofocus.Autofocus.clear
documentation: Remove all registration points.

methods.name: pystages.autofocus.Autofocus.focus
documentation:
Guess the correct focus depth given abscissa and ordinate of a new
point. This method will raise a RuntimeError if not enough points have
been registered.

**Warning: Always check for the returned value bounds to prevent stage
dangerous displacements. Bad points registration or bugs can lead to
unexpected big displacements.**

```
param x

Abscissa of the point.

param y

Ordinate of the point.

return

Calculated depth.
```

methods.name: pystages.autofocus.Autofocus.__focus_3
documentation:
Guess correct focus using 3 points.
\:param x: Abscissa of the point.
\:param y: Ordinate of the point.
\:return: Calculated depth.

methods.name: pystages.autofocus.Autofocus.__len__
documentation:
---
return: Number of registered points.
---

Utility class to add autofocus support for scanning software. Given a set
of focused coordinates, this class can calculate the correct Z-depth for
other points.

Triggers a Home command.

```
param wait

```

methods.name: pystages.tic.Tic.go_home
documentation:
Run the homing procedure.
\:param direction: Homing direction.
\:param wait: If True, wait for homing procedure end.

methods.name: pystages.tic.Tic.position
documentation:
Motor position, in steps.

:getter: Returns current target position.
:setter: Set target position and wait until position is reached.

methods.name: pystages.tic.Tic.is_moving
documentation:
Because there is no specific command to get the current moving state of the Tic, and
the positioning/homing is always blocking, this function returns False in all cases.

Very basic driver class for Polulu Tic Stepper Motor controller, connected in USB.

:ivar poll_interval: Interval between successive state polling for some
 long motor operations.

documentation: Possible statuses that the CNC can report.

classes.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCError
documentation: Exception raised when a specific error is detected by the CNC

classes.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.__init__
documentation:
Open serial device to connect to the CNC routers. Raise a
ConnectionFailure exception if the serial device could not be open.

```
param dev

Serial device. For instance . If not provided, try
to discover a suitable device according to device vendor and product IDs

param reset_wait_time

Depending on the state of the stage, it can take some time for
GRBL to reset. This parameter makes the wait time to be tuned, by giving a time in seconds.
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.reset_grbl
documentation:
Sends a CTRL+X control to reset the GRBL

```
return

True if the GRBL sent the correct prompt at the end of the reset

param wait_time

Depending on the state of the stage, it can take some time for GRBL to
reset. This parameter makes the wait time to be tuned, by giving a time in seconds.
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.sleep
documentation:
Sends a `$SLP` command. The stage responds a message `[MSG:Sleeping]` after `ok`.

```
return

The response of the CNC ('ok' if command has been submitted correctly).
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.unlock
documentation:
Unlock the motor. It may happen when the stage has gone further its limits,
and raised an alarm, or has been disabled when going in sleep mode (`$SLP`)

```
return

 if message  has been returned
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.set_grbl_setting
documentation:
Set the GRBL setting of the Router with given value. The value type must correspond to
type defined in {py:class}`GRBLSetting`.

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.get_grbl_settings
documentation:
Obtains and parse the list of GRBLSettings with the `$$` command.

```
return

A dictionary containing the GRBLSetting as key and its corresponding value
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.get_current_status
documentation:
Sending '?' character permits to get the status of the CNC
router

```
return

A tuple containing the status and a dictionary of all other parameters in the
output of the command.
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.send
documentation:
Send a command.

```
param command

Command string.

param eol

End of command character(s). If None, LF will be automatically append.
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.receive_lines
documentation:
Receive multiple lines until getting as specific value.

```
param until

The expected response indicating the end of received lines.

return

The list of all received lines. Note that the expected line is not included in the
list.
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.receive
documentation:
Read input serial buffer to get a response. Blocks until a response is
available.

```
return

Received response string, CR-LF removed.
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.send_receive
documentation:
Send a command, wait and return the response.

```
param command

Command string.

return

Received response string, CR-LF removed.
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.position
documentation:
Current stage position. Vector.

:getter: Query and return stage position.
:setter: Move the stage.

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.is_moving
documentation:
Queries the current status of the CNC in order to determine if the CNC is moving

```
return

True if the CNC reports that a cycle is running (Run) or
if it is in a middle of a homing cycle (Home).
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.set_origin
documentation:
Set current position as origin

```
return

The response of the CNC ('ok' if command has been submitted correctly).
```

methods.name: pystages.cncrouter.CNCRouter.home
documentation:
Sends a `$H` command. The stage responds a message `[MSG:Sleeping]` after `ok`.
Take caution for collisions before calling this method !

```
param wait

Optionally waits for move operation to be done.
```
Class to command CNC routers.

classes.name: pystages.smc100.Link

methods.name: pystages.smc100.Link.__init__
documentation:
---
param dev: |-
  Serial device. For instance `'/dev/ttyUSB0'`.
  If not provided, a suitable device is searched according to
  according to vendor and product IDs
---

methods.name: pystages.smc100.Link.send
documentation:
Send a command to a controller.

```
param address

Controller address. If None, only the command is sent without prefix.

param command

Command string. Don't include address nor CR LF since
they are added automatically by this method.
```

methods.name: pystages.smc100.Link.receive
documentation:
Read input serial buffer to get a response. Blocks until a response is
available.

```
return

Received response string, CR-LF removed.
```

methods.name: pystages.smc100.Link.query
documentation:
Send a query.

```
param address

Controller address. int.

param command

Command string, without '?'.

param lazy_res

If True, response is not fetched. The caller must
ensure the response will be fetched later. This flag can be used
for optimization to send a batch of queries and then fetch all the
response.

return

Received response, or None if lazy_res is True.
```

methods.name: pystages.smc100.Link.response
documentation:
Get and return the response of a query. Parameters are required to
check the header of the response.

```
param address

Controller address. int.

param command

Command string, without '?'.
```

Class to control Newport SMC100 controllers. This uses a serial device to
communicate with multiple controllers which may be configured in daisy-chain
configuration.

Current design allows multiple instance of SMC100 to share the same
communication channel without being considered as the same stage. For
example, we can have two XY stages using four SMC100 controllers connected
in daisy-chain configuration.

As specified in SMC100 controllers documentation, the address of the first
controller in the daisy chain is always zero.

classes.name: pystages.smc100.State
documentation:
Possible controller states.
The values in this enumeration corresponds to the values returned by the
controller.

classes.name: pystages.smc100.Error
documentation: Information returned when querying positioner error.

classes.name: pystages.smc100.ErrorAndState

methods.name: pystages.smc100.ErrorAndState.is_referenced
documentation:
---
return: True if state is not one of the NOT_REFERENCED_x states.
---

methods.name: pystages.smc100.ErrorAndState.is_ready
documentation:
---
return: True if state is one of READY_x states.
---

methods.name: pystages.smc100.ErrorAndState.is_moving
documentation:
---
return: True if state is MOVING.
---

methods.name: pystages.smc100.ErrorAndState.is_homing
documentation:
---
return: True if state is one of HOMING_x states.
---

methods.name: pystages.smc100.ErrorAndState.is_jogging
documentation:
---
return: True if state is one of JOGGING_x states.
---

methods.name: pystages.smc100.ErrorAndState.is_disabled
documentation:
---
return: True if state is one of DISABLE_x states.
---
Information returned when querying positioner error and controller state.

classes.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.__init__
documentation:
---
param addresses: An iterable of int controller addresses.
param dev: |-
  Serial device string (for instance `'/dev/ttyUSB0'` or
  'COM0'), an instance of Link, or an instance of SMC100 sharing
  the same serial device.
  If not provided, a suitable device is searched according to
  according to vendor and product IDs
---

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.position
documentation:
Stage position, in micrometers.

:getter: Query and return current stage position.
:setter: Move stage.

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.home
documentation:
Perform home search.

```
param wait

Optionally waits for move operation to be done.
```

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.home_search
documentation:
Perform home search.
Home search is performed even if the axes are already referenced.
It may be better to use home_search_if_required.

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.home_search_if_required
documentation: Perform home search for all axes which are not referenced.

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.get_error_and_state
documentation:
Query current motion controller errors and state.
Querying the error and state may clear error flags.

```
param addr

Address of the axis.

return

Current error and state, in a ErrorAndState instance.
```

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.enter_configuration_state
documentation: Enter configuration state.

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.leave_configuration_state
documentation:
Leave configuration state. If defined parameters are valid, the
controller saves them in the flash memory.

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.controller_address
documentation: Get controller's RS-485 address. int in [2, 31].

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.set_controller_address
documentation:
Set controller's RS-485 address. int in [2, 31].
Changing the address is only possible when the controller is in
configuration state.

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.move_relative
documentation:
Moves relatively an axis from a given offset

```
param addr

addr of axis to move

param offset

offset value
```

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.stop
documentation:
Stops the motion on an axis. On all axis if addr not specified.

```
param addr

Address of the axis to stop. If None, stop all the controllers
```

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.reset
documentation:
Resets the controller at specified address. For all controllers if not specified

```
param addr

address of the controller to reset
```

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.set_position
documentation:
Sets the position of a single axis

```
param addr

address of the axis to set the position

param value

stage position, in micrometers

param blocking

if True, blocking mode: wait for the position to be
reached before exit.
```

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.is_moving
documentation:
Indicates if the stage is currently moving due to MOVE, HOME or JOG operation.

```
return

Moving state of the stage
```

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.is_disabled
documentation:
Indicates if the stage is currently in DISABLE state.
\:return: Disabled state of the stage

methods.name: pystages.smc100.SMC100.enter_leave_disable_state
documentation:
Permits for a specified axis to enter or leave the DISABLE state.
DISABLE state makes the motor not energized and opens the control loop.

```
param addr

address of the axis to operate.
If None is passed, it applies to all controllers

param enter

True to enter, False to leave DISABLE state
```
Class to command Newport SMC100 controllers.

methods.name: pystages.m3fs.M3FS.__init__
documentation:
Connect to the device. If the serial device cannot be opened, a
ConnectionFailure exception is thrown. If the device version is not
supported, a VersionNotSupported error is thrown.

```
param dev

Serial device. For instance .
If not provided, a suitable device is searched according to
according to vendor and product IDs

param baudrate

Serial baudrate.
```

methods.name: pystages.m3fs.M3FS.__send
documentation:
Send a command to the controller.

```
param command

Command integer ID.

param data

Extra data string.
```

methods.name: pystages.m3fs.M3FS.__receive
documentation:
Read next response from the controller.

```
return

Response string, without '<', '>' and carriage return.
```

methods.name: pystages.m3fs.M3FS.command
documentation:
Send a command to the controller and get the response.

```
param command

Command integer ID.

param data

Extra data string.

return

Response data string.
```

methods.name: pystages.m3fs.M3FS.__get_closed_loop_status
documentation:
Query closed-loop status and position.

```
return

Tuple of 3 int.
```

methods.name: pystages.m3fs.M3FS.position
documentation:
Stage position, in micrometers.

:getter: Query and return current stage position.
:setter: Move stage. Wait until position is reached.

Class to command New Scale Technologies M3-FS focus modules in serial mode
(VCP).
If a M3-FS device is seen as a USBXpress chip, it may be used as a
Virtual-Com-Port after a few cumbersome operations (custom driver with
correct VID/DID).

classes.name: pystages.stage.Stage

methods.name: pystages.stage.Stage.__init__
documentation:
---
param num_axis: |-
  The number of axis of the stage, can be updated or set after initialization
  of the object.
---

methods.name: pystages.stage.Stage.find_device
documentation:
Find automatically one device according to given information in parameters

```
param pid

Product ID of the device. If None, not a limiting criteria.

param vid

Vendor ID of the device. If None, not a limiting criterial.

param serial_number

serial number of the device. If not None, the device
must have a serial number and matches the given value.

return

The device path to the communication port.
```

methods.name: pystages.stage.Stage.position
documentation:
Current stage position. Vector.

:getter: Query and return stage position.
:setter: Move the stage.

methods.name: pystages.stage.Stage.position
documentation: Set stage to a new destination.

methods.name: pystages.stage.Stage.is_moving
documentation: Queries the status of the stage to determine if it is moving

methods.name: pystages.stage.Stage.wait_move_finished
documentation: Wait until all axis are ready.

methods.name: pystages.stage.Stage.move_to
documentation:
Move stage to a new position, with the possibility to poll device until the move finishes.

```
param value

New stage position.

param wait

If True, wait for stage to be at the new position,
otherwise return immediately.
```

methods.name: pystages.stage.Stage.check_dimension
documentation:
Check if the given value's dimension is consistent according to the number of axes of the
Stage.
\:param value: The vector to check
\:raises ValueError: If dimension is inconsistent with the number of axis of the Stage.

methods.name: pystages.stage.Stage.check_range
documentation:
Check if the given value is in range according to minimums/maximums.
If it is not the case, a ValueException is raised.

```
param value

The vector to check if it is within authorized range.
```

methods.name: pystages.stage.Stage.home
documentation:
Triggers a homing command.

```
param wait

Optionally waits for move operation to be done.
```

Stage is an abstract class from which all stage implementation must inherit to get a consistent
behavior and value checking for getting and setting position, setting software limits...

classes.name: pystages.vector.Vector

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: Initial values of the vector.
param dim: Dimension of the vector.
---

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.x
documentation: First element of the vector.

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.y
documentation: Second element of the vector.

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.z
documentation: Third element of the vector.

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.w
documentation: Fourth element of the vector.

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.xy
documentation: First and second elements of the vector, as a 2D Vector.

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.__getitem__
documentation:
---
param key: int or slice.
return: |-
  Vector element if key is an integer, or a list of items if key
  is a slice.
---

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.__setitem__
documentation:
Set an item of the vector.
\:param key: int or slice.
\:param value: New value.

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.__str__
documentation:
---
return: String representation of the vector. For instance '(1, 2, 3)'.
---

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.__add__
documentation:
---
param other: A Vector instance of same dimension.
return: Sum of this vector to the other.
---

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.__sub__
documentation:
---
param other: A Vector instance of same dimension.
return: Difference between this vector and the other.
---

methods.name: pystages.vector.Vector.__mul__
documentation:
---
param other: A Vector instance of same dimension, or an integer or a float.
return: Scalar multiplication between this vector and the other.
---
Some basic vector manipulation class for stages control.
